seoTitle: How to Give Customer Statements in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Send customer statements from QuickBooks Online to keep clients informed on balances, transactions, and outstanding payments.
intro: Customer statements keep clients informed about their transaction history and any outstanding balance, which helps maintain trust and speeds up payment. This guide covers generating, customizing, and sending customer statements in QuickBooks Online.

steps.heading: Navigate to the customer statements feature
steps.body: Open the area in QuickBooks Online dedicated to generating customer statements.

steps.heading: Select the customer or customers
steps.body: Choose which customer accounts you want to generate statements for, whether individually or in bulk.

steps.heading: Customize the statement
steps.body: Adjust the statement to include the relevant date range, transaction details, and any branding elements.

steps.heading: Review the statement for accuracy
steps.body: Check that transaction history and balances are correctly reflected before sending.

steps.heading: Send or automate delivery
steps.body: Send the statement directly to the customer, or set up automated delivery for recurring statement schedules.

faq.question: How often should I send customer statements?
faq.answer: This depends on your business, but many companies send statements monthly or whenever there's a significant outstanding balance.

faq.question: Can I automate customer statement delivery?
faq.answer: Yes, QuickBooks Online allows you to set up automated statement delivery to save time on recurring client communications.

faq.question: What information is typically included in a customer statement?
faq.answer: A customer statement usually includes transaction history, payment history, and any current outstanding balance.

faq.question: Can customer statements help improve payment collection?
faq.answer: Yes, keeping clients regularly informed of their balance can encourage timely payments and reduce disputes over amounts owed.
